title: The Lansdowne MS of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales
summary: The General Corpus artifact is the pinned agz8236 XML, copied from University of Michigan item AGZ8236.0001.001. U-M made its uncorrected OCR and Level-1 TEI representation in 2006 from Frederick J. Furnivall's seven-part Chaucer Society edition of British Library Lansdowne MS 851. Furnivall transcribed the principal sequence from Lansdowne but supplied explicitly marked readings and appendix passages from twelve other English witnesses, retained Tyrwhitt's paragraph divisions in Melibee and the Parson's Tale, quoted Middle French comparison passages from Royal MS 19 C XI, and advertised visual material copied from the Ellesmere and Cambridge Gg.4.27 manuscripts. Seven complete BSB part bundles, later Thomson research files, Tyrwhitt scans, three complete public supporting-manuscript facsimiles, and two complete scans proving both advertised plate suites were issued in Furnivall's later 1885 Harley MS 7334 volume are cached separately; current public facsimiles were not verified for Lansdowne or the other supply witnesses.
review_notes: The primary lineage subject is the checked-in XML artifact; the metadata cataloging unit is Furnivall's manuscript-centered edition-level compilation.
Lansdowne MS 851 supplies the continuous base sequence, but every explicitly supplied reading is represented by its own passage-scoped relation.
Royal MS 19 C XI contributes Middle French comparison quotations only. It is not an English Canterbury Tales witness and does not make Middle French a work-level discovery language.

The exact BSB Part III carrier contains fourteen Ellesmere-derived woodcuts. Furnivall's appendix contents additionally advertises twenty-three Ellesmere tellers and six tellers plus six allegorical figures from Cambridge Gg.4.27; the latter advertised set was not found in the exact BSB part carriers.
Public access, private cache presence, copyright status, provider terms, and permission to publish are recorded independently for every layer.

entities.label: British Library Lansdowne MS 851
entities.description: Furnivall's principal witness. The official record dates this English parchment Canterbury Tales manuscript to c. 1410 and locates the Tales at ff. 2r-255r, with Gamelyn at ff. 54v-65r. Its sequence is A-B alpha-F(Squire)-D-E-F(Franklin)-G-C-B beta-H-I. One lost leaf in quire xxx accounts for the Parson's Tale supply from Royal MS 18 C II.

holding.notes: Acquired by the British Museum with the Lansdowne collection in 1807.

physical_description.extent: 255 folios plus flyleaves and a stub
physical_description.dimensions: 340 x 210 mm; written space approximately 250 x 140 mm
notes: Written in England in Gothic cursive/Bastard Secretary; the official description associates the language with the Gloucestershire-Worcestershire-Herefordshire border area.

editorial_practices.source_feature: gaps, omitted lines, and variant passages in Lansdowne MS 851
editorial_practices.representation: bracketed or source-labeled readings from twelve English supply witnesses
editorial_practices.description: The edition is not homogeneous: source labels and manuscript milestones identify every continuous or isolated supply modeled in the relation graph.
editorial_practices.scope: Wife, Squire, Clerk, Merchant, Franklin, Summoner, Second Nun, Pardoner, Nun's Priest, Melibee, Parson, and appendix passages

editorial_practices.source_feature: Lansdowne has no paragraph breaks in Melibee or the Parson's Tale
editorial_practices.representation: Tyrwhitt's paragraph divisions retained in print
editorial_practices.description: Furnivall says the retained divisions prevent small Six-Text differences from displacing many lines in Melibee and are kept for convenience in the Parson's Tale.
editorial_practices.scope: Melibee and the Parson's Tale only

editorial_practices.source_feature: Middle French Melibee in Royal MS 19 C XI
editorial_practices.representation: five source-language comparison quotations in the Melibee apparatus
editorial_practices.description: The French passages are quoted for comparison and do not replace the Middle English base text.

editorial_practices.source_feature: teller portraits and allegorical figures in Ellesmere and Cambridge Gg.4.27
editorial_practices.representation: woodcut copies and an advertised appendix drawing set
editorial_practices.description: The exact BSB Part III carrier contains fourteen selected Ellesmere-derived cuts; appendix p. 701 advertises broader Ellesmere and Gg.4.27 figure sets. Both complete suites are confirmed in two later 1885 Harley-edition carriers, while their accompaniment or completeness in the earlier Lansdowne issue remains open.

editorial_practices.source_feature: graphemic and layout features of Lansdowne MS 851
editorial_practices.representation: line-by-line legacy Word transcription using custom tags and a custom font mapping
editorial_practices.description: The deposited introduction documents the transcription model, but the bundled font member is zero bytes, limiting exact recovery of some special characters.

evidence.citation: Structured inspection of the complete agz8236 XML against all seven complete BSB Furnivall part carriers.
evidence.locator: bound title sequence 19; printed pp. 1-700; appendix contents pp. 701-702; appendix pp. 1*-8*
evidence.summary: The carrier sequence is a seven-part, Lansdowne-centered scholarly compilation: twenty-four conventional Canterbury Tales tales in A-Bα-F(Squire)-D-E-F(Franklin)-G-C-Bβ-H-I order, a non-counted Gamelyn, marked supplies and quotations, a Christ Church link on p. 700, and source-specific appendix matter. XML counts are 756 PB, 757 EPB, 18,361 raw L, 622 LG, 215 direct P, 55 DIV2, and 64 DIV3; twenty-five L occur inside notes and 18,336 are direct non-note lines.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Lansdowne MS 851, record 040-002078368.
evidence.locator: identity, date, contents, physical description, provenance, access, and digital-image status
evidence.summary: Official record for the principal witness: English parchment codex, c. 1410, 255 folios plus flyleaves and a stub, Canterbury Tales and Gamelyn at ff. 2r-255r, manuscript sequence and missing quire-xxx leaf, former Lansdowne No. 907, and current statement that digital images are unavailable. It requires a letter of introduction for consultation.

evidence.citation: Christ Church Library, MS 152 catalog description.
evidence.summary: Official manuscript description supporting shelfmark, fifteenth-century date, contents, and holding. The Nun's Priest end-link locus is verified directly against the complete IIIF object.

evidence.citation: Christ Church Library, Special Collections reader information and Western Manuscripts collection.
evidence.summary: Official institution-specific route for external researchers to request an advance appointment using an exact manuscript reference; MS 152 is identified in the Western Manuscripts collection.

evidence.citation: Christ Church Library, Imaging Services and Photography.
evidence.summary: Official image-supply and reproduction-permission route, including contact and permission requirements. It supports requesting existing or new images without implying redistribution rights.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian, Christ Church MS 152, complete IIIF manifest.
evidence.locator: 684 canvases; target at f. 173v/canvas 348 and f. 174r/canvas 349
evidence.summary: Complete provider sequence. The first twelve end-link lines are on canvas 348 and the final four on canvas 349; the manifest states that manuscript images are all rights reserved by Christ Church.

evidence.citation: Integrity and target-locus audit of the complete Christ Church MS 152 IIIF bundle.
evidence.summary: All 684 provider canvases are present; ZIP CRC, inventory, hashes, and target surface mapping were verified. Complete means the complete published provider object, not only Furnivall's two source surfaces.

evidence.citation: The Huntington Library, Ellesmere Chaucer, EL 26 C 9, item 2838 and IIIF manifest.
evidence.locator: 480 published canvases; fourteen confirmed Furnivall source portraits and twenty-three announced portraits mapped at discontiguous folio/canvas loci
evidence.summary: Complete published provider object for the Ellesmere manuscript. The fourteen cuts visible in Furnivall Part III and the full twenty-three-image announcement were cross-mapped to current Huntington folios and canvases; the provider sequence omits four cataloged non-text leaves.

evidence.citation: Integrity, inventory, and target-locus audit of the complete published Huntington Ellesmere object.
evidence.locator: confirmed fourteen-cut subset at canvases 26, 74, 89, 99, 104, 132, 159, 167, 181, 293, 303, 309, 342, and 364; twenty-three announced portraits mapped in the manifest
evidence.summary: All 480 provider canvases are present; ZIP CRC, inventory membership, every recorded image hash, and JPEG decoding were rechecked after hardlink reuse. Completeness is scoped to the provider inventory, while target relevance is only the discontiguous visual-source set.

evidence.citation: The Huntington Library, rights and permissions guidance.
evidence.summary: The public IIIF delivery does not display an affirmative open image license; provider rights and permission guidance therefore remains applicable to redistribution and publication.

evidence.citation: Cambridge Digital Library, MS Gg.4.27(1), complete viewer and IIIF manifest.
evidence.locator: 1,144 canvases; nine advertised visual-source leaves at ff. 186r/c383, 192v/c396, 222r/c455, 306r/c623, 352r/c715, 395r/c801, 416r/c843, 432r/c879, and 433r/c881
evidence.summary: Complete provider object for the entire current Gg.4.27(1) anthology. The nine exact illustrated leaves underlie six teller and six allegorical figures announced for Furnivall's appendix, but no derived Gg plate suite was found in the seven checked BSB carriers.

evidence.citation: Integrity, inventory, and visual-locus audit of the complete CUDL MS Gg.4.27(1) object.
evidence.locator: Reeve c383; Cook c396; Wife of Bath c455; Pardoner c623; Monk c715; Manciple c801; Envy/Charity c843; Gluttony/Abstinence c879; Lechery/Chastity c881
evidence.summary: All 1,144 provider canvases are present; ZIP CRC, inventory membership, all recorded image hashes, and JPEG decoding were rechecked after hardlink reuse. Direct visual inspection confirmed the nine discontiguous source leaves and the paired allegorical figures; every image used the 1600-pixel profile.

evidence.citation: Furnivall's 1885 Harley MS 7334 edition, complete Harvard/Google scan at Internet Archive (harleianmschauc01chaugoog).
evidence.locator: 799 PDF pages; Ellesmere title PDF 719 and all 23 cuts on odd PDFs 721-765; Gg title PDF 767 and design-bearing surfaces PDFs 769, 771, 773, 775, 777, 787, 789, 791, 793
evidence.summary: Direct page-by-page inspection confirms the complete twenty-three-cut Ellesmere suite and the complete Gg suite of six teller designs plus six paired allegorical figures in this 1885 Harley carrier. PDFs 779, 781, and 783 repeat the Pardoner surface and PDF 785 repeats Monk; they are scan states, not additional designs. This proves later 1885 issuance only and does not establish presence in the earlier Lansdowne carriers.

evidence.citation: Furnivall's 1885 Harley MS 7334 edition, second complete Google scan at Internet Archive (harleianmschauc00chaugoog).
evidence.locator: 800 PDF pages; Ellesmere title PDF 721 and all 23 cuts on odd PDFs 723-767; all Gg designs on PDFs 769, 771, 773, 775, 777, 779, 781, 783, 785
evidence.summary: Independent page-by-page inspection confirms both complete visual suites. PDF 787 repeats the Canon's Yeoman scan and is not another Gg design. Agreement between the two complete carriers resolves whether the suites were issued by 1885 while leaving their earlier Lansdowne-edition carrier history open.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright, April 2026.
evidence.summary: Official U.S. term guidance supporting public-domain treatment of works published before 1931 as of 2026. It does not determine provider contract terms or rights in modern image derivatives.

evidence.citation: UK Intellectual Property Office, Copyright Notice: Duration of Copyright.
evidence.summary: Official UK term guidance supporting separation of already-published medieval readings from residual manuscript content whose valid publication history is unestablished, including transitional protection that may continue through 31 December 2039 for some works unpublished at the end of 1988.

open_questions.question: Whether and where did Furnivall's complete advertised Ellesmere and Gg.4.27 visual suites accompany the earlier Lansdowne edition before their confirmed appearance in his 1885 Harley MS 7334 volume?
next_steps: Search earlier Chaucer Society issue wrappers, plate lists, separately issued portfolios, and Lansdowne bound copies beyond the seven exact BSB carriers.
Compare any located earlier carrier with the two independently verified 1885 Harley carriers and retain copy-specific plate order.
Keep the Lansdowne Gg.4.27 relationship as a qualified citation or announcement until an earlier issued carrier is inspected.
notes: Resolved portion: both complete advertised suites were issued by Furnivall's later 1885 Harley MS 7334 volume and survive complete in two inspected scans. Unresolved portion: no complete suite was located in the seven checked earlier Lansdowne BSB part carriers, which contain only fourteen Ellesmere-derived cuts plus the announcements.
